evals.name: deploy-existing-config-success
evals.prompt: Deploy this Scrapy project to Scrapy Cloud from /tmp/my-scrapy-project
evals.expected_output: Skill verifies shub and auth, finds project root with scrapy.cfg, detects valid scrapinghub.yml with project configured, runs shub deploy, and reports deployed version, spiders count, and project link
assertions: Runs `shub --version` first
Checks authentication via `$SHUB_APIKEY` and/or `~/.scrapinghub.yml` before deploy
Validates project root by confirming `scrapy.cfg` exists
Uses existing `scrapinghub.yml` when `project` or `projects.default` is present
Runs `shub deploy` from the project root
Success report includes Version, Spiders, and project URL `https://app.zyte.com/p/<project_id>/`

evals.name: install-shub-when-missing
evals.prompt: Deploy my Scrapy project to Zyte Cloud
evals.expected_output: When shub is not installed, skill installs it with `uv tool install shub`, then continues with auth checks and deployment steps
assertions: If `shub --version` fails with command not found, runs `uv tool install shub`
Does not stop after installation and continues with auth checks
Proceeds to locate project root and run `shub deploy`

evals.name: auth-missing-runs-login
evals.prompt: Push my spider project to Scrapy Cloud
evals.expected_output: If no API key is found in environment or config, skill runs interactive `shub login` and then proceeds with deploy flow
assertions: Checks `echo "${SHUB_APIKEY}"`
Checks `~/.scrapinghub.yml` for an `apikey` if env var is empty
Runs `shub login` only when both checks show missing credentials
Mentions signing up at `https://app.zyte.com` if the user has no account

evals.name: create-scrapinghub-yml-from-scratch
evals.prompt: Deploy /tmp/my-scrapy-project to Scrapy Cloud; it does not have scrapinghub.yml yet
evals.expected_output: Skill asks for numeric project ID, discovers requirements file if available, resolves latest stack tag from Docker Hub tags, writes scrapinghub.yml, then deploys
assertions: If `scrapinghub.yml` is missing, opens `https://app.zyte.com/o/projects/` for the user
Asks user for numeric project ID
Detects dependency file using precedence `requirements.txt`, then `pyproject.toml`, then `Pipfile`
Selects stack tag in format `{VERSION}-{YYYYMMDD}` and writes `stack: scrapy:<tag>`
Creates `scrapinghub.yml` with `project` and optional `requirements.txt` file
Runs `shub deploy` after writing config

evals.name: wrong-project-id-retry
evals.prompt: Deploy my project and fix any project-id issues automatically
evals.expected_output: If deployment fails with 'Project N does not exist', skill diagnoses wrong ID, updates scrapinghub.yml with correct project ID, and retries deployment
assertions: On deploy failure, surfaces the full error message
Recognizes `Project N does not exist` as wrong project ID
Fixes `scrapinghub.yml` project value before retry
Retries `shub deploy` after correction

evals.name: requirements-path-fix
evals.prompt: Deploy and handle requirements file errors
evals.expected_output: If deploy fails with missing requirements file, skill corrects `requirements.txt` file path in scrapinghub.yml and retries deploy
assertions: Recognizes `Could not find requirements file` error
Updates `requirements.txt` file path in `scrapinghub.yml`
Retries deployment after fixing the path
If additional errors remain, reports full message and asks how to proceed

evals.name: schedule-basic
evals.prompt: Schedule myspider on Scrapy Cloud using shub
evals.expected_output: Skill verifies shub and auth, runs `shub schedule myspider`, and reports the returned job ID plus the web UI job link
assertions: Runs `shub --version` before scheduling
Checks authentication via `$SHUB_APIKEY` and `~/.scrapinghub.yml`
Schedules with `shub schedule myspider`
Output includes job ID in format `PROJECT_ID/SPIDER_ID/JOB_ID`
Output includes the web UI job link `https://app.zyte.com/p/PROJECT_ID/job/SPIDER_ID/JOB_ID`

evals.name: schedule-with-args-settings-priority-units
evals.prompt: Run myspider with start_url=https://example.com, LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG, priority 3, and 3 units
evals.expected_output: Skill maps options to `shub schedule` flags using `-a`, `-s`, `-p`, and `-u`
assertions: Uses `-a start_url=https://example.com`
Uses `-s LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG`
Uses `-p 3`
Uses `-u 3`
Keeps command in `shub schedule` form (not HTTP API curl)

evals.name: schedule-specific-project
evals.expected_output: Skill targets a non-default project by prefixing spider with project id (`33333/myspider`)
assertions: Runs `shub schedule 33333/myspider` when explicit project is requested
Does not rely only on local `scrapinghub.yml` default project

evals.name: view-job-items
evals.expected_output: Skill opens the Scrapy Cloud items page in the browser for job 12345/2/15
assertions: Opens browser with URL matching pattern `*/p/12345/2/15/items`
URL respects `SCRAPY_CLOUD_ENDPOINT` — uses `https://app-staging.zyte.com/p/12345/2/15/items` when endpoint is set to staging
Does not attempt to download items via curl or HTTP API

evals.name: view-job-log
evals.expected_output: Skill opens the Scrapy Cloud log page in the browser for job 12345/2/15
assertions: Opens browser with URL matching pattern `*/p/12345/2/15/log`
URL respects `SCRAPY_CLOUD_ENDPOINT` — uses `https://app-staging.zyte.com/p/12345/2/15/log` when endpoint is set to staging
Does not attempt to download logs via curl or HTTP API

evals.name: spider-not-found-guidance
evals.prompt: Schedule spider notarealspider and handle errors
evals.expected_output: If scheduling fails with spider-not-found style error, skill explains likely wrong spider name or missing deployment and advises deploying first with `shub deploy`
assertions: Recognizes spider-not-found errors as name/deployment issue
Recommends verifying spider name
Recommends deploying project first using `shub deploy`

evals.name: list-running-jobs
evals.prompt: List all running jobs for project 12345
evals.expected_output: Skill uses curl against the Jobs API to list running jobs, respecting the SCRAPY_CLOUD_ENDPOINT environment variable
assertions: Uses curl with `-u "$SHUB_APIKEY":` for authentication
Calls `${SCRAPY_CLOUD_ENDPOINT:-https://app.zyte.com/api/}jobs/list.json` with `project=12345&state=running`
With staging endpoint set, calls `https://app-staging.zyte.com/api/jobs/list.json`
Does not use shub for listing jobs

evals.name: stop-job
evals.expected_output: Skill uses curl to POST to the stop endpoint, passing the correct project and job parameters
assertions: Uses curl with `-u "$SHUB_APIKEY":` for authentication
POSTs to `${SCRAPY_CLOUD_ENDPOINT:-https://app.zyte.com/api/}jobs/stop.json`
Passes `-d project=12345` and `-d job=12345/2/15`
With staging endpoint set, calls `https://app-staging.zyte.com/api/jobs/stop.json`

evals.name: item-stats
evals.prompt: How many items did job 12345/2/15 scrape? What fields does it have?
evals.expected_output: Skill calls the items stats endpoint and reports total item count and per-field counts
assertions: Calls `${SCRAPY_CLOUD_STORAGE_ENDPOINT:-https://storage.zyte.com/}items/12345/2/15/stats`
With staging storage endpoint set, calls `https://storage-staging.zyte.com/items/12345/2/15/stats`
Reports `totals.input_values` as the total number of items
Reports per-field counts from the `counts` object
Does not open the browser — stats are fetched directly via API
